title: GLM-5.2 targets long-horizon coding with 1M-token context
friendlyTitle: A new open AI model is built for long coding tasks
summary: Z.AI introduced GLM-5.2, an MIT-licensed open model focused on long-horizon coding, agentic tasks, and reliable 1M-token context. The release highlights architecture changes such as IndexShare to reduce long-context compute and improve sustained coding-agent performance.
friendlySummary: GLM-5.2 is an open model designed to handle very long coding and agent tasks. It claims a large context window and better efficiency for long work sessions.
whyItMatters: Open models are increasingly competing on the kinds of messy, multi-step engineering tasks that used to favor closed frontier systems. That gives developers and companies more leverage on cost, deployment control, and data-governance choices.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Open models can give builders more control and lower costs. This matters when companies want powerful AI without sending every task to a closed platform.
